title: JavaScript Introduction Knowledge Check
description: Check your understanding of JavaScript basics, history, runtime behavior, setup, first scripts, and syntax rules.
author: DailyCoder

questions.prompt: Which technologies are mentioned as the three foundational technologies of the World Wide Web?
questions.explanation: JavaScript, HTML, and CSS are commonly described as the three foundational technologies of the World Wide Web.

questions.prompt: What does it mean that JavaScript is dynamically typed?

options.text: Variables must always be declared with a fixed type
Variable types are determined at runtime and can change
JavaScript code must be compiled manually before running

questions.explanation: In JavaScript, you do not declare fixed variable types. A variable can hold different kinds of values as the program runs.

questions.prompt: Which programming styles does JavaScript support?
questions.explanation: JavaScript is multi-paradigm, so it supports imperative, object-oriented, and functional programming styles.

questions.prompt: How can JavaScript handle events and asynchronous work without freezing the application?

options.text: By using multiple call stacks for every task
By blocking the browser until each task finishes
By using the Event Loop and browser or runtime APIs
By requiring every function to run on a server

questions.explanation: JavaScript has one call stack, but the Event Loop and APIs provided by the browser or runtime allow asynchronous work to be handled efficiently.

questions.prompt: What does single-threaded mean in the context of JavaScript?

options.text: JavaScript can execute only one piece of code on the call stack at a time
JavaScript always runs faster than compiled languages

questions.explanation: JavaScript is single-threaded because it uses one call stack, so it executes one piece of code at a time while asynchronous work is coordinated through the Event Loop and runtime APIs.

questions.prompt: Who created the first prototype of JavaScript in 1995?
questions.explanation: Brendan Eich created the first JavaScript prototype at Netscape in May 1995.

options.text: A JavaScript framework for building user interfaces
The official standard that JavaScript follows
A browser extension for running JavaScript

questions.explanation: ECMAScript is the official language standard. JavaScript is the most widely used implementation of that standard.

questions.prompt: What did Node.js make possible for JavaScript developers?

options.text: Making JavaScript the same language as Java

questions.explanation: Node.js lets JavaScript run outside the browser, which made it useful for servers, command-line tools, build tools, and more.

questions.prompt: Which tools are part of a typical beginner JavaScript setup? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: A code editor such as VS Code
A modern web browser with Developer Tools
A C++ compiler before every script can run

questions.explanation: A code editor, browser DevTools, and Node.js/npm form a practical JavaScript setup. You do not need a C++ compiler to run JavaScript.

questions.prompt: Which HTML tag is commonly used to connect an external JavaScript file to a webpage?
questions.explanation: The script tag with a src attribute tells the browser to load and run an external JavaScript file.

options.text: Prints output to the developer console or terminal

questions.explanation: console.log() prints values to the browser developer console or to the terminal when running with Node.js.

questions.prompt: Which identifier name is valid in JavaScript?
questions.explanation: userName is valid and follows camelCase. Identifiers cannot start with a number, include hyphens, or use reserved keywords like let.

questions.prompt: JavaScript treats `score` and `Score` as the same variable name.
questions.explanation: JavaScript is case-sensitive, so score and Score are different identifiers.

questions.prompt: Which of these are JavaScript literals? (Select all that apply.)
questions.explanation: Strings, numbers, booleans, arrays, and objects can all be written directly as literals in JavaScript code.

questions.prompt: What is the purpose of comments in JavaScript?

options.text: They are executed before the rest of the program
They leave notes for humans and are ignored by the engine
They convert JavaScript into machine code

questions.explanation: Comments are notes for developers. The JavaScript engine ignores them when running the program.
